channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI investment isn't slowing down — venture capitalists are funding startups while trying to grapple with environmental impact
item.description: Generative AI is rapidly expanding, attracting significant investment while raising concerns about its environmental impact.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla is against the NHTSA’s crash reporting rule. The Trump transition team wants to scrap it
item.description: The Trump transition team wants the incoming administration to drop a car-crash reporting requirement opposed by Elon Musk’s Tesla, according to a document seen by Reuters, a move that could cripple the government’s ability to investigate and regulate the safety of vehicles with automated-driving sy
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: For December’s Patch Tuesday, 74 updates and a zero-day fix for Windows
item.description: Microsoft released 74 updates in its December Patch Tuesday update, with patches for Windows, Office and Edge — but none for Microsoft Exchange Server or SQL server.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: YouTube is taking over TV
item.description: YouTube was launched in February 2005 and soon adopted a simple motto: “Broadcast Yourself.” While we initially watched on PCs, and then smartphones, it seems that these days an increasing number of us are engaging with YouTube content on our televisions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump’s inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google CEO eyes AI 'Manhattan Project' after Trump inauguration
item.description: Alphabet and Google CEO Sundar Pichai said in a recent interview that he is eyeing a research project on artificial intelligence (AI), similar to the Manhattan Project during World War II, after President-elect Trump returns to the White House next month.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: If you invested in Apple’s IPO 44 years ago, here’s what your shares would be worth today
item.description: On December 12, 1980, a few hours after the opening bell rang on Wall Street, shares of Apple Computer officially began trading. The numbers, by today’s standards, weren’t that impressive.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft Softens Stance on Windows 11 Upgrade Requirements
item.description: Microsoft offers a concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don't meet minimum hardware requirements, but warns of potential risks and lack of support.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Helen King’s job is all about keeping AI safe as Google scales
item.description: More than a dozen years ago, U.K.-based game producer Helen King was about to take a new job at Ubisoft in Canada. She had already boxed up her possessions and was getting her visa in order when an opportunity at a London-based AI startup unexpectedly presented itself.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: How to choose videoconferencing software
item.description: When most businesspeople think of videoconferencing software, the first thing that comes to mind is probably Microsoft Teams or Zoom, but there are many other choices, each with its own strengths.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: InfoWorld’s 2024 Technology of the Year Award winners
item.description: Artificial intelligence, including generative AI, continues to evolve rapidly across enterprise organizations, despite a lot of heavy lifting still required of software developers and data scientists.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response
item.description: When it comes to cybersecurity, the more data you have, the easier it is to identify anomalies and stop adversaries, shares Agriculture CISO Ignatius Liberto. The post Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Android 2025 preview: 7 things to watch
item.description: As we look ahead to a whole new year of green-tinted Googley goodness, I’m feeling both guardedly excited — and curiously like Bill Murray in the movie Groundhog Day. Google’s got some pretty weighty pivots in the works for the Android arena right now.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Databricks unveils synthetic data generation API to help evaluate agents faster
item.description: As enterprise demand for building multi-agent systems continues to grow, data infrastructure services providing firm, Databricks, is updating its Mosaic AI Agent Evaluation module with a new synthetic data generation API that will help enterprises evaluate agents faster.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5G never delivered for cloud computing
item.description: I recall this as if it was yesterday. I entered the conference room to talk about cloud computing and 5G. I gave this type of presentation often a few years ago. The executives were excited about 5G’s potential. I took a deep breath, knowing I had to voice my concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX
item.description: Michelyne LeBlanc, the deputy CIO of the Defense Commissary Agency, said the move to zero trust is all about ensuring customer confidence in its services. The post Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
